overall_assessment: The implementation is structurally close, but the redirect allowlist has a blocking ordering flaw: invite-code canonicalization can happen before external URL rejection. That contradicts the frozen contract's external-path fallback requirement and creates drift between the public resolver and the private allowlisted resolver.
recommended_path: Fix before passing T1: make invite-code extraction subject to the same relative-path guard as the continuation allowlist, then add focused tests for absolute and scheme-relative invite URLs across resolveWebPromotionPath and buildTenantPromotionUri.

findings.title: Blocking: external invite URLs bypass the redirect allowlist
findings.rationale: resolveWebPromotionPath calls resolveWebPromotionShareCode before the relative-url guard in _resolveAllowedPromotionRedirectPath, and buildTenantPromotionUriFromAppContext uses the same share-code helper before allowlist resolution. Because resolveWebPromotionShareCode does not reject uri.hasScheme, uri.hasAuthority, or scheme-relative input, a redirectPath such as https://evil.example/invite?code=CODE123 is canonicalized as a valid /invite handoff instead of falling back to /. This violates the frozen contract requirement that external paths fall back, and it is an elegance/structural problem because invite handling now has a separate less-strict path from the canonical continuation allowlist.
findings.suggested_action: Move the relative-path rejection into resolveWebPromotionShareCode or route all invite-code extraction through a single guarded helper. Add tests proving absolute and scheme-relative /invite?code=... inputs fall back for both resolveWebPromotionPath and buildTenantPromotionUri.
